reviews.review_description: I havent seen anything else like this. Good graphics good gameplay turned based rpg in vr

The game was confusing at first as the tutorial is very poor, but once you get past the poor tutorial you realise the game is a bit simple and repetitive.  The graphics are very basic and need updating for Quest 3.  You also need a lot of space to get the best out of it.
Great game now has a great demo available. This FREE demo has hours of content in it. Check it out, what are you waiting for???
Great concept and even for beta it’s a pretty refined game. Love the art style.

I have spent around 3 hours playing and this is just the demo!! I will definitely be buying the full game!!!

The gameplay is super addictive and the combat system is amazing. Dodging enemy attacks in room scale feels so good and the intense boss fights are just so immersive and pure fun. I played on hard difficulty and it took a good 6/7 tries to beat the second boss

I am excited to buy and play the full game!!!

reviews.review_title: This is the RPG I was looking for
reviews.review_description:
Arcaxer is a unique VR RPG that has first person combat, and 3rd person god like mode when you explore the overworld and dungeons. 

This is a turn based RPG that hits all the key elements. Three classes to choose from, leveling system with stat upgrades. Find and sell loot, buy new gear, upgrades and items. Upgrade your gear and weapons, learn new skills and hax (magic). Procedural dungeons to explore, and much more.

I love using the room scaling to move around the dungeons and over world to explore and see all the little details. The combat with room scaling is amazing as well, and helps draw you into the VR world. 

This is an VR RPG you don't want to miss. The VR experience is just amazing and you don't want to pass it by. Just download the free demo and you will be hooked.

reviews.review_title: Great rpg with tons of replayability
reviews.review_description: I can’t explain much, you’d just have to get the game for yourself. (There is a dog!) amazing, 10/5 stars.

This one completely flew under my radar but I finally tried it and oh my Goodness it is pretty fun and we'll made.

I very well may have to throw down for the full version after playing this demo for a couple hours straight.  The Quest needs more RPGs and this one is well worth trying out.
Pretty cool concept, and really fun to boot!
Ye verry good game for a demo anyway so its worth a try
It lacks a little instruction but it was a really nostalgic execution of both 3rd and 1st person turn based RPGs. I hope to see additional levels soon.
If you're thinking about downloading this demo, do it.
Interesting gameplay. It’s moss meets dungeon crawler, meets rpg, meets arcade. I loved it and can’t wait for the full version.
good

reviews.review_title: This was made by a cool gamer
reviews.review_description:
It has all the features I would've wanted if i could make a game. Retro JRPG mechanics in VR space. The demo lets you do so much and try everything. If you’re a fan of turn based JRPG you’ve gotta give this a try. 

One really cool thing Ive noticed is the status effects are very well implemented into VR. When enemy cast a blind/vision dimming spell you literally have trouble seeing their attacks. That’s so cool!
Is there a way to actually get that chest? Just curious haven't been able to get it and my curiosity is getting the better of me ATM. Phenomenal game by the way.

reviews.review_title: Omg i will be buying this as SOON asap
reviews.review_description:
I have only played the demo but WOW!!!
The graphics. The gameplay. The interface. The controls. Everything about this game is a 5/5. Highly recommend it for anyone who loves fantasy and tabletop games but would like to be IN the game and see from the pov of the character, as well as 3rd per pov.... hard to explain, just try it yourself. Does NOT disappoint

cool stuff, gotta buy it. $25 for a game is a lot of money for me right now, since we are at war and it would be better to donate it to the army (the headset was given to me), but I want to buy this. Maybe when we win...

once you get the hoverboard, try going into first person while riding it. you can actually still control it and it's cool as heck.

Oh, and the RPG stuff is good too.

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I was looking for
